name: A Good Deal
content: Deal with my feelings I must and write them down. It is said that putting words to paper memorializes them for eternity. We burn the books we write.

I have lost my voice. Doctor increased my dose of Bupropion XL from 150 milligrams first to 300, and then to 450—not 451; I do not burn. My throat is dry as a leaf in Fall. I can speak to an audience for five minutes before my lips start to stick to each other. I do not speak to a woman for longer than ten. It is somewhat funny, really: I crave not poison but elixir. Mornings begin with pills and potions and nights end in smoke.
I can not say that drugs do not work. I have done my laundry before it transcends its basket. I have put bedsheets to mattress. That is the assignment, and it is complete and content.
I stand in front of an arc of men, equidistant from each. I cannot sing. They smile and I leave.
